sections.heading: This Act may be cited as the Superior Courts (Number of Short title
In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires— Interpretation
sections.text: “Chief Justice” means the Chief Justice appointed in accordance with Article 140 of the Constitution; Cap. 1 “Constitutional Court” means the Constitutional Court established under Article 127 of the Constitution; Cap. 1 “Court of Appeal” means the Court of Appeal established under Article 130 of the Constitution; Cap. 1 “High Court” means the High Court established in accordance with Article 133 of the Constitution; Cap. 1 “Deputy Chief Justice” means the Deputy Chief Justice appointed in accordance with Article 140 of the Constitution; Cap. 1 Single copies of this Act may be obtained from the Government Printer, P.O. Box 30136, 10101 Lusaka, Price K 4.00 each. 176 No. 12 of 2025] Superior Courts (Number of Judges) “Deputy Judge President” has the meaning assigned to the Act No. 7 of words in the Court of Appeal Act, 2016; 2016 “Deputy President of the Constitutional Court” means the Deputy President of the Constitutional Court appointed Cap. 1 under Article 140 of the Constitution; “judge” has the meaning assigned to the word in the Cap. 1 Constitution and the word “judges” shall be construed accordingly; “Judge President” has the meaning assigned to the words in Act No. 7 of the Court of Appeal Act, 2016; 2016 “superior court” has the meaning assigned to the words in the Cap. 1 Constitution; and “Supreme Court means the Supreme Court established under Cap. 1 Article 124 of the Constitution; Supreme 3. There shall be thirteen judges of the Supreme Court, including Court the Chief Justice and the Deputy Chief Justice. judges

sections.heading: There shall be thirteen judges of the Constitutional Court,
sections.text: Constitutional Court judges including the President of the Constitutional Court and the Deputy President of the Constitutional Court. Court of 5.
